summary_text: { "scores": { "accuracy": 1, "performance": 1, "maintainability": 1 }, "total": 3, "issues": [ { "severity": "critical", "file": "scripts/fugue-lane-bridge.mjs", "line": "180-227, 579-586", "description": "Assumption: matrix lanes are intended to update shared state safely under parallel execution. That is not true here. `handleMatrix()` runs lanes concurrently via `Promise.allSettled(...)`, while each lane calls `updateProviderState()`, which does an unlocked read-modify-write of `fugue-provider-state.json`. Concurrent completions race, and the last writer wins, dropping provider/lane updates from other lanes. This makes provider-state persistence incorrect exactly in the matrix path the bridge is meant to support.", "suggestion": "Separate impact scopes: (1) minimal fix: stop sharing one JSON document and write per-run/per-lane append-only records, then derive status from aggregation; (2) if the single file must remain, add cross-process locking or serialize all state writes in the parent after lane completion instead of from concurrent lane tasks." }, { "severity": "critical", "file": "scripts/fugue-lane-bridge.mjs", "line": "230-237, 320-336", "description": "Assumption: `task`, child stdout, and stderr may contain credentials, tokens, or other sensitive material. The bridge persists raw `stdout`, `stderr`, and full `args` to `~/.claude/cache/lane-outputs/*.json` without any redaction. `args` includes `-t <task>`, so secrets embedded in prompts are written to disk even when child scripts try to redact console output. This violates the stated security requirement (`no secrets leak`) and creates a durable local secret leak.", "suggestion": "Split remediation by blast radius: (1) immediate containment: stop persisting raw `stdout`, `stderr`, and `args` entirely, or store only bounded/redacted summaries; (2) defense-in-depth: run the same DLP/redaction pass used by delegate scripts before any persistence, and avoid storing the raw task string unless explicitly required." } ], "summary": "`spawn()` wiring, failover invocation, diversity gate, summary budget on bridge stdout, and 0/1/2 return paths are mostly coherent. The two critical failures are shared-state corruption in parallel matrix runs and unredacted on-disk persistence of potentially secret-bearing task/output data." }
